relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul’s sending is not human-sourced but resurrection-sourced.
The siblings with him address the Galatian assemblies.
Grace and peace open the freedom letter.
Messiah gives himself to rescue from the present harmful age.
Glory returns to the Divine into the ages.
Paul marvels that they are being transferred from grace to a distorted message.
The rival message is not true good news but trouble and distortion.
Even angelic brilliance cannot override the received good news.
Anything beyond the received freedom-message falls under judgment.
Servanthood to Messiah cannot be built on human approval.
The good news Paul announces is not human-manufactured.
It came through unveiling of Jesus Messiah.
Paul remembers his former ravaging of the assembly.
His former zeal exceeded many of his peers.
Grace had already marked him from the womb.
The Son was unveiled in him for announcement among the nations.
He did not immediately seek authorization from flesh and blood.
Years later he visited Cephas in Jerusalem.
He also saw James, the Lord’s brother.
He writes before the Divine without lying.
His path moves through Syria and Cilicia.
The Judean assemblies in Messiah did not know his face.
They heard the former pursuer now announces the trust he ravaged.
They glorified the Divine in him.

contemplative_note: Galatians 1 is the alarm bell: freedom can be distorted quickly. The true source is not performance, approval, or institutional possession, but the unveiling of Messiah in a transformed life.
